condition: Both satisfy the Senior Project I eligibility 'one of' requirement, but they are not interchangeable as general degree requirements outside that specific gate; an advisor must confirm which requirement is being satisfied.
Senior Project I and II are sequential capstone courses; II requires I as a prerequisite, so neither substitutes for the other.
Calculus II requires Calculus I as a prerequisite; it is a continuation, not a substitute.
Core-curriculum requirements in different skill domains (English vs Arabic language); not interchangeable.
Different core-curriculum package requirements (Knowledge & Skills vs Natural Science/Math); each satisfies a distinct requirement slot.
Different core-curriculum package requirements (Humanities/Fine Arts vs Social & Behavioral Sciences); each satisfies a distinct requirement slot.
Computer Ethics (major core, 1 CH) and Islamic Culture (core curriculum, 3 CH) satisfy different requirement categories entirely.
Both are required college-level lab science courses; neither waives the other.
Data Structures and Algorithms are sequential (Algorithms requires Data Structures); neither substitutes for the other.
Both are generic major-elective slots filled from the same approved elective pool (see _elective_pool_reference in courses.json); any approved elective may fill either slot.
Linear Algebra and Probability & Statistics cover different math foundations; an advisor must confirm program-specific mapping before allowing this as a substitution.
